health_zone_table.basis: SitRep33 section 3.2 per-health-zone cumulative confirmed-case list, cross-checked to Table 1 and the INSP banner 875/202. The PDF does not publish per-zone confirmed-death counts this cycle; confirmed deaths are therefore retained only at province/national rollup and as an explicit unallocated residual, not fabricated by zone. Promotion captured source-direct from the INSP PDF because INRB-UMIE had not yet mirrored SitRep 33 at review time (PDF sha256 b70cd26f0bceba7ed91ac86dfbeaec776106385885536ce383d368068a6eb6cf).

reconciliation.note: Do not allocate the 94 unventilated confirmed cases or the 202 national confirmed deaths to named health zones; SitRep33 does not publish current per-zone deaths.

review_corrections: SitRep33 names two newly affected health zones: Fataki (Ituri) and Musienene (Nord-Kivu).
DATA-SOURCE VARIABILITY: INRB-UMIE had not yet exposed a SitRep33 raw PDF/processed per-zone table at review time; the INSP PDF is the source-direct primary evidence for this promotion.

The SitRep33 per-zone section is a narrative cumulative confirmed-case list, not a per-zone confirmed-death table. Per-zone deaths are not inferred; the national 202 confirmed deaths remain an unallocated residual for the zone-death surface.
The explicit Ituri ventilation note remains 94 additional confirmed cases in course of ventilation.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: 371 new contacts were listed in seven Ituri health zones: Rwampara 100, Bunia 86, Mongbwalu 81, Nyankunde 54, Aungba 34, Lita 14, Mambasa 2.

challenges.finding: Reluctance to post-mortem swabbing can delay confirmation and underestimate cases.
challenges.response: Intensify community engagement and involve religious leaders.

challenges.finding: Normed CTE capacity remains insufficient despite the Rwampara opening, creating potential saturation pressure.
Contact follow-up remains below target at 71.6% against a 95% target, compromising interruption of transmission chains.
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays.

challenges.finding: Essential generic medicines are insufficient in targeted HGRs, weakening care continuity.
IPC materials are insufficient in Nord-Kivu, increasing nosocomial risk.
Alert reporting remains weak in Sud-Kivu, delaying case detection.
Insufficient resources across all pillars slow operations; the SitRep cites a USD 21.5M gap.

priorities.priority: Reinforce community sensitization and engagement of local leaders
Brief providers on MVE surveillance and SBC in affected and high-risk zones
Brief Ituri community agents on MVE surveillance and SBC
Continue supervised support of national and provincial coordination across technical pillars
Catch up contact follow-up in Ituri and Nord-Kivu
Reinforce IPC and staff training in Nyankunde and other nosocomial zones
Deploy a daily standardized KPI dashboard by pillar
Execute the Nord-Kivu pending-sample analysis catch-up plan

publication_context: INSP SitRep 33 reports data through 2026-06-16 and states publication 2026-06-17; the INSP WordPress post appeared on 2026-06-18.
The outbreak is reported across 3 provinces and 33 of 104 health zones.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 799 of 875 DRC confirmed cases (91.3%).
Nord-Kivu stands at 73 confirmed cases and carries the highest provincial confirmed-case fatality ratio at 58.9%.
Sud-Kivu has reported no new confirmed case since 2026-05-26.
highlights: 40 new confirmed cases including 4 confirmed deaths were reported for 2026-06-16.
New confirmed cases were reported in Ituri (Rwampara 13, Bunia 11, Mongbwalu 4, Tchomia 3, Fataki 1) and Nord-Kivu (Butembo 4, Katwa 2, Oicha 1, Musienene 1).
Two new health zones were touched: Fataki in Ituri and Musienene in Nord-Kivu.
18 patients were declared recovered, bringing cumulative recoveries to 67.
surveillance: 737 alerts were reported and 475 investigated, producing a 64.5% investigation rate.
192 suspected cases of the day were validated, including 46 deaths.
6,856 contacts were under follow-up and 4,910 were seen, producing 71.6% follow-up coverage across the three provinces.
371 new contacts were listed in seven Ituri health zones.
care_continuity: End-of-day isolation stood at 379 patients (152 confirmed, 227 suspected) at 87.5% occupancy.
18 new recovered patients included a 16-month-old child; three recovered patients were people deprived of liberty.
laboratory: SitRep33 does not publish the national sample-collected/sample-analyzed/positive testing table used in prior cycles.
The SMSPS table reports laboratory results announced to patients, but those values are not promoted as national testing throughput.
redactions: Page contact details (named INSP officials and their phone and email) are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.

preserved_conflicts: SitRep33 headline is DRC-only (875 confirmed / 202 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope public snapshot adds the latest reviewed Uganda component separately.
SitRep33 section 3.2 includes an explicit unventilated Ituri residual of 94 confirmed cases; these counts are public but not mappable to named health zones.
SitRep33 does not publish current per-zone confirmed-death counts; per-zone deaths are not inferred from province totals or prior cycles.
Daily suspected cases (192) and suspected deaths (46) are current-cycle validated alert outputs, not cumulative suspected stock or cumulative suspected deaths.
INRB-UMIE had not yet exposed the SitRep33 raw PDF/processed tables at review time; the INSP PDF is the primary source for this cycle.

The SitRep33 confirmed-count banner carries an asterisk noting figures are being harmonized to integrate confirmed cases still in the community and escaped cases; the 875 figure carries that source caveat.

review_notes: SitRep #033 (data cutoff 2026-06-16; PDF states publication 17 June 2026; INSP WordPress post 25023 appeared 2026-06-18T12:28:50Z). PDF sha256 b70cd26f0bceba7ed91ac86dfbeaec776106385885536ce383d368068a6eb6cf, text sha256 515f0030bca15ed22588800135d32e075bbf4ee3193bbc0ea0d6a6f20993e5b8, captured source-direct from the INSP PDF because INRB-UMIE had not yet mirrored SitRep 33.

DRC headline 875 confirmed / 202 confirmed deaths; +40 confirmed and +4 confirmed deaths vs SitRep32 (data 2026-06-15). Ituri reported 32 new confirmed including 1 death; Nord-Kivu reported 8 including 3 deaths.
New confirmed cases were Rwampara 13, Bunia 11, Mongbwalu 4, Tchomia 3, Fataki 1, Butembo 4, Katwa 2, Oicha 1, and Musienene 1.

Two newly affected health zones were named: Fataki (Ituri) and Musienene (Nord-Kivu). Gazetteer centroids were added from the INRB/GRID3 DRC health-zone shapefile, matching the existing polygon-centroid convention.

Section 3.2 reconciles named zones 781 confirmed plus the explicit unventilated Ituri residual 94 confirmed to the 875 DRC banner. Per-zone confirmed-death counts are not published in SitRep33 and are not inferred; the 202 national deaths remain an unallocated zone-death residual.

Uganda anchor carried unchanged at 19 confirmed / 2 deaths / 5 recovered after MoH /api/stats showed zero new cases through 2026-06-18; country-scope = 894 confirmed / 204 confirmed deaths / 72 recovered.
Laboratory throughput was not promoted: SitRep33 lacks the prior national samples-collected/analyzed/positive table. SMSPS-announced-result rows are not equivalent to national testing throughput.

Operational data captured: 737 alerts (475 investigated, 64.5%), 192 suspected cases of the day (46 deaths), 6,856 contacts under follow-up (71.6%), 379 in isolation (152 confirmed / 227 suspected, 87.5% occupancy), 371 new contacts listed in seven Ituri zones, PoC activation 80.0% (44/55) with 54,660 travelers passed.

Source caveat: the confirmed-count banner carries an asterisk noting data harmonization to integrate confirmed cases still in the community and escaped cases; suspected deaths in the community remain under investigation for possible reclassification.
